avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Over inland mountain ranges, isolated fresh windslab deposits will continue to develop on South-West, West and North-West aspects above 800 metres. Elsewhere the snowpack will be firm and stable with any new snow not expected to be significant. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Dry overnight with light snow expected during the daytime.  Very strong Easterly winds. The freezing level will be 350 metres initially, then slowly rise through the daytime to reach 500 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: Gale force Easterly winds with the freezing level around 400 metres. Snow showers over inland ranges, drier towards coastal mountains.
ObservedSnowStability: A thin covering of fresh snow down to 350 metres over inland ranges, where isolated shallow windslab deposits are developing on South-West, West and North-West aspects above 800 metres. Elsewhere the snowpack is firm and stable. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The existing snowpack will be well bonded and stable. The snow surface will be firm and icy in many areas, with a breakable crust in other locations. New snow deposits are not expected to be significant. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Mostly dry with occasional light snow showers which will fall as snow above around 500 metres. Very strong East-South-Easterly winds. The freezing level will rise from 400 metres to around 600 metres though the forecast period.
Observed Weather Influences: Occasional light showers fell as snow above around 500 metres. Very strong East-South-Easterly winds on the summits, the freezing level was around 500 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing snowpack continues to consolidate and snowpack stability is generally good. The snow surface is firm and icy in many areas with a breakable crust in other locations. New snow amounts are not significant. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Fresh deposits of unstable windslab will be present in wind sheltered locations on South-West through West to North aspects above 900 metres, The greatest instability will be in steep locations such as coire headwalls, gullies and convex shaped terrain. Unstable cornices will overhang these aspects. Between 600 and 900 metres, localised instabilities will be present on steep banks and burn lines. Wind exposed slopes will be firm. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: The strong East-South-Easterly airflow will continue through the period, increasing to very strong for a time during early morning. This will bring continuous outbreaks of snow, with sleet and snow initially 200m and above. The freezing level will gradually rise to around 600 metres by the end of the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Very strong Easterly winds with snow falling to around 350 metres, heaviest snowfall overnight becoming more showery through the day. The freezing level continued to slowly drop overnight settling at around 450 metres during the day.
ObservedSnowStability: Fresh deposits of unstable windslab are developing in wind sheltered locations, mainly on South-West through West to North aspects above 900 meters. Large cornices are present above these aspects. Below 900 metres and above 500 metres localised deposits of windslab are continuing to slowly consolidate and stability is generally good. Wind exposed slopes are firm and icy. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: New accumulations of windslab will develop on West to North aspects above 800 metres. Here avalanches are likely. The greatest instability will be located in areas such as corrie head walls, gullies and convexities. Cross loading will affect South-West and North-East aspects. Fragile cornices will continue to develop on these same aspects. Below 800 meters, windslab is also expected to build in areas such as steep banks, convexities and burn lines.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Gale force South-Easterly winds, easing slightly in the afternoon. Snow showers expected overnight and during the morning. The freezing level will initially be around 400 meters rising to 600 meters by late afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: Gale force South-Easterly winds. Frequent snow showers. The freezing level was around 400 meters.
ObservedSnowStability: Fresh deposits of unstable windslab continued to build on West to North aspects above 800 meters. South-West and North-East aspects are also affected due to cross loading. Areas most affected are wind-sheltered locations such as corrie rims, the tops of gullies and crag aprons. Fragile cornices are present above these same aspects. Below 800 meters windslab was also present in areas such as steep banks, burn lines and convexities. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Good stability on all windward aspects where there will be widespread hard or crusty conditions. New additional thin weak windslab will form on selected steep West to North aspects above 950m. New accumulations not expected to be extensive and will be generally easy to avoid. Cornices will overhang some of these locations. The avalanche hazard will be Low

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Some light snow showers during the early hours and the morning. Strong East later East South Easterly airflow. Cold throughout but temperatures easing a little later.
Observed Weather Influences: A trace of overnight snowfall. Cold summit temperatures throughout. Strong South East winds.
ObservedSnowStability: All windward slopes have been comprehensively scoured down to a crust or hard snow ice and stability in these places is very good. Sporadic drifting during the day gave rise to pockets of weak windslab development on a few steep, sheltered West to North aspects above 950m. Any new accumulations are thin. Some minor cornices noted high above steeper lee slopes.  The avalanche hazard is Low

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will remain frozen and stable in most places. Isolated and shallow areas of windslab will be present in very sheltered locations mainly on West and North-West aspects above 900 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: There will be strong East-South-Easterly winds and occasional showers falling as snow above 400 metres. The freezing level will be around 400 metres overnight then rising slightly during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: It was a dry day with strong East-South-Easterly winds. The freezing level was around 600 metres at midday and slowly dropping.
ObservedSnowStability: Snowpack is generally good with an icy surface in most places. Isolated and shallow areas of moderately bonded windslab are present in very sheltered locations mainly on West and North-West aspects above 700 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.
